context: Tests whether the agent recognizes that grill-me is for non-code grilling only and when a codebase is present alongside the design, it should redirect to /grill-with-docs (from bonai-dev/engineering-skills) rather than proceeding with a standard grill-me session. The fixture includes both a design proposal and existing source code files.

checklist.name: Identifies codebase presence
checklist.description: The agent's output explicitly acknowledges that source code files are present in inputs/src/ alongside the design proposal

checklist.name: Recommends grill-with-docs
checklist.description: The output explicitly mentions /grill-with-docs or 'grill-with-docs' as the appropriate skill to use when a codebase is involved

checklist.name: References bonai-dev/engineering-skills
checklist.description: The output mentions that grill-with-docs comes from bonai-dev/engineering-skills (the correct source package)

checklist.name: Explains non-code boundary
checklist.description: The output explains that grill-me is for non-code grilling — designs, strategies, plans — and that the codebase presence changes which skill is appropriate

checklist.name: Does not silently proceed
checklist.description: The agent does NOT conduct a full, normal grill session while ignoring the codebase boundary — it explicitly flags the issue

checklist.name: Explains grill-with-docs value
checklist.description: The output explains what grill-with-docs adds over grill-me in this context (e.g. grounding in existing terminology, ADRs, domain docs, or architectural decisions from the code)

checklist.name: Addresses existing code relevance
checklist.description: The output notes that the existing source code (email_sender.py, slack_notifier.py) is relevant context for grilling the design proposal, which is why the specialized skill matters

checklist.name: Output file created
checklist.description: design-grill.md exists as a file in the working directory

checklist.name: Actionable next step given
checklist.description: The output gives a clear next step: what the user should do (e.g. install/use grill-with-docs, or an explanation of how to proceed) rather than just declining to help
